req.title: Interest accrual
req.statement: A savings account shall accrue interest daily using the tier rate and credit interest monthly on the last business day.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-016 mandates daily interest accrual using tier rates and monthly crediting on the last business day via /cron/interest. Tests cover: (1) EP positive – a standard mid-tier balance account receives correct interest credit and ledger entry on a normal last-business-day trigger; (2) NEG negative – triggering the job on a non-last-business-day must not credit interest, exercising the error/guard path; (3) BVA boundary – an account balance sitting exactly on a tier boundary (e.g. $10,000.00) to validate rate selection at the edge; (4) DT decision-table – a zero-balance account where no interest should accrue and no ledger entry should be written; (5) STC state-transition – verifying the account moves from ACCRUING state to INTEREST_CREDITED state after the month-end job fires, and returns to ACCRUING for the next cycle.

tests.name: test_interest_credited_on_last_business_day_standard_balance
preconditions: A savings account exists with a balance in the mid-tier range (e.g. $5,000.00)
Daily accrual records exist for every calendar day of the current month
Today is the last business day of the month
actions: Seed account via <seed_account_fixture> with balance=5000.00 and 30 daily accrual rows
POST /cron/interest with a date header matching the last business day
Fetch the account ledger via GET /accounts/{account_id}/ledger
expected: POST /cron/interest returns HTTP 200
Response body contains account_id with status=CREDITED
Ledger contains exactly one INTEREST_CREDIT entry for the month
Credited amount equals sum of daily accruals rounded to 2 decimal places
trace: R-FIN-016: interest credited monthly on the last business day
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from decimal import Decimal

BASE_URL = "<BASE_URL>"

@pytest.fixture
def standard_account(seed_account_fixture):
    return seed_account_fixture(balance=5000.00, accrual_days=30, tier="mid")

def test_interest_credited_on_last_business_day_standard_balance(
    standard_account, last_business_day_date
):
    account_id = standard_account["account_id"]
    expected_interest = standard_account["expected_interest"]

    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/cron/interest",
        json={"run_date": last_business_day_date},
        headers={"X-Idempotency-Key": f"test-ep-{account_id}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    body = resp.json()
    result = next(r for r in body["results"] if r["account_id"] == account_id)
    assert result["status"] == "CREDITED"

    ledger_resp = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/accounts/{account_id}/ledger")
    entries = [e for e in ledger_resp.json()["entries"] if e["type"] == "INTEREST_CREDIT"]
    assert len(entries) == 1
    assert Decimal(str(entries[0]["amount"])) == Decimal(str(expected_interest))

tests.name: test_interest_not_credited_on_non_last_business_day
preconditions: A savings account exists with a balance of $5,000.00
Daily accrual records exist for the partial month
Today is NOT the last business day of the month (e.g. mid-month date)
actions: POST /cron/interest with a date header set to a mid-month date
Fetch the account ledger via GET /accounts/{account_id}/ledger
expected: POST /cron/interest returns HTTP 200 (job ran) or HTTP 422/409 (guard rejection)
Response body status for account is NOT CREDITED (e.g. SKIPPED or absent)
No INTEREST_CREDIT ledger entry is created for the account
No reconciliation variance is reported for this account
trace: R-FIN-016: credit interest monthly on the last business day (guard: no credit on other days)
R-FIN-016: error pathway – reconciliation variance reported if credited incorrectly

tests.name: test_interest_accrual_at_tier_boundary_balance
preconditions: Two savings accounts exist: one at exactly $10,000.00 (tier boundary) and one at $9,999.99 (just below)
Daily accrual records exist for the full month for both accounts
Today is the last business day of the month
actions: Seed two accounts via <seed_account_fixture>: balance=10000.00 and balance=9999.99
POST /cron/interest with last-business-day date for both accounts
Fetch credited amounts from ledger for both accounts
expected: Account at $10,000.00 is credited using the UPPER tier rate
Account at $9,999.99 is credited using the LOWER tier rate
Both credits are present as INTEREST_CREDIT ledger entries
The $10,000.00 account credit amount is strictly greater than the $9,999.99 account credit (proportionally)
trace: R-FIN-016: accrue interest using the tier rate
R-FIN-016: tier boundary at $10,000.00 separates rate bands
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from decimal import Decimal

BASE_URL = "<BASE_URL>"
TIER_BOUNDARY = Decimal("10000.00")

@pytest.fixture
def boundary_accounts(seed_account_fixture):
    acc_at = seed_account_fixture(balance=10000.00, accrual_days=30, tier="upper")
    acc_below = seed_account_fixture(balance=9999.99, accrual_days=30, tier="lower")
    return acc_at, acc_below

def test_interest_accrual_at_tier_boundary_balance(
    boundary_accounts, last_business_day_date
):
    acc_at, acc_below = boundary_accounts

    for acc in (acc_at, acc_below):
        resp = requests.post(
            f"{BASE_URL}/cron/interest",
            json={"run_date": last_business_day_date},
            headers={"X-Idempotency-Key": f"test-bva-{acc['account_id']}"}
        )
        assert resp.status_code == 200

    def get_credit(account_id):
        r = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/accounts/{account_id}/ledger")
        entries = [e for e in r.json()["entries"] if e["type"] == "INTEREST_CREDIT"]
        assert len(entries) == 1
        return Decimal(str(entries[0]["amount"]))

    credit_at = get_credit(acc_at["account_id"])
    credit_below = get_credit(acc_below["account_id"])

    rate_at = credit_at / TIER_BOUNDARY
    rate_below = credit_below / Decimal("9999.99")
    assert rate_at > rate_below, "Upper tier rate must exceed lower tier rate at boundary"

tests.name: test_no_interest_credited_for_zero_balance_account
preconditions: A savings account exists with a balance of exactly $0.00
No positive daily accrual records exist for the month
Today is the last business day of the month
actions: Seed account via <seed_account_fixture> with balance=0.00 and accrual_days=30
Fetch ledger for the zero-balance account
expected: POST /cron/interest returns HTTP 200
Account status in response is SKIPPED or ZERO_BALANCE
No INTEREST_CREDIT ledger entry is created
No reconciliation variance is raised for this account
trace: R-FIN-016: tier rate applied – zero balance maps to zero interest (DT: balance=0 row)
R-FIN-016: ledger entry per account – no entry when credit amount is zero

tests.name: test_account_state_transitions_through_accrual_and_credit_cycle
preconditions: A savings account exists in ACTIVE state with balance $2,500.00
The account has no prior accrual records for the current month
The banking-api exposes account state via GET /accounts/{id}/state
actions: POST /cron/daily-accrual for each day of the month to drive state to ACCRUING
Assert account state is ACCRUING after first accrual
POST /cron/interest on the last business day to trigger credit
Assert account state transitions to INTEREST_CREDITED
POST /cron/daily-accrual for the first day of the next month
Assert account state returns to ACCRUING
expected: After daily accrual: account state is ACCRUING
After month-end interest job: account state is INTEREST_CREDITED
After next month first accrual: account state is ACCRUING (cycle resets)
Each state transition is recorded with a timestamp in the audit log
trace: R-FIN-016: accrue interest daily (ACTIVE -> ACCRUING transition)
R-FIN-016: credit interest monthly on last business day (ACCRUING -> INTEREST_CREDITED)
R-FIN-016: system job trigger drives cycle reset
